kept.text: "En los últimos meses, la economía de la IA ha dado un vuelco. Anthropic, antes conocida como la peculiar y obsesionada con la seguridad competidora de OpenAI, se ha convertido posiblemente en la empresa de más rápido crecimiento en la historia del capitalismo. Sí, lo ha leído bien. En los primeros tres meses de este año, los ingresos de Anthropic crecieron cuatro veces más rápido que los de Google en tres años durante su período de máximo crecimiento. La demanda de los productos de Anthropic crece tan rápido que la empresa se ve obligada a restringir el servicio a los usuarios durante las horas pico porque no puede garantizar la potencia informática suficiente para satisfacerla. Y aunque Anthropic destaca claramente, casi todas las demás empresas del sector de la IA, incluidos los proveedores de servicios en la nube y los fabricantes de chips, también están creciendo a una velocidad vertiginosa". Escribe hoy @TheAtlantic

quoted_tweet.text:
New Anthropic Fellows research: Model Spec Midtraining (MSM).

Standard alignment methods train AIs on examples of desired behavior. But this can fail to generalize to new situations.

MSM addresses this by first teaching AIs how we would like them to generalize and why.

kept.text:
Anthropic刚刚宣布：和Blackstone、Goldman Sachs、Apollo等华尔街巨头成立一家15亿美元的合资公司。

目标是什么？把AI工程师直接植入中型企业的核心运营。

这不是卖软件。这是去抢麦肯锡的生意。

---

全球管理咨询行业规模超过3000亿美元。这个行业的商业模式非常简单：你有一个问题，我们派一组聪明的顾问，他们在你公司待几个月，交一份报告，收几百万美元。

Anthropic的判断是：那份报告现在可以由AI生成。那几个月可以压缩到几周。那几百万美元可以降到几十万。

如果他们是对的，这是一场对咨询行业的系统性冲击。不是优化，是替代。

---

值得注意的是：OpenAI在做同样的事，和TPG、Bain Capital谈类似的结构。

两家AI公司同时向同一个方向进攻，用的是同一套逻辑。

这一般意味着：方向是对的。

https://t.co/LqM1rtXjjm

Anthropic is giving away 3 Claude certifications. 

All free. Here are the exact links:

1. Claude 101 - 1 hour. The basics, done right.
2. AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations - 3 hours.
3. Intro to Cowork - 2 hours. Claude's best feature.

All 3 are on https://t.co/gn6AikWqGM. 
Sign-up takes 30 seconds.

Tons of people buy $200 "Claude guides" online.

Very few know Anthropic gives the real ones away.

Somehow, most people blame the job market... when the problem is what's on their LinkedIn.

I made a full guide to all 3 official certifications, in the right order, with my notes on each one.

It's free if you click here: https://t.co/ClEur2jjxA.

Forget paying. Just know which ones are real.

---

PS: I am not paid by Claude.
I know I talk (a whole lot) about Claude. But I don't care about Claude. Claude doesn't pay me.

I'm sharing, twice a week, how my work is speeding up (very fast) with AI.

As I'm trying to keep up, I want 𝘺𝘰𝘶 to keep up. So we move just as fast.

I want to be the great filter to your AI noise. And 574,000 people read this twice a week to focus on the how.

kept.text:
Osa sijoittajista pelkää OpenAI:n ja/tai Anthropicin tilauskirjan pitävyyttä. Tämä pelko on turha maailmassa, jossa laskentateho on pullonkaula. 

OpenAI ja Anthropic eivät oikeastaan ole edes loppuasiakkaita, vaan välikerros, jonka takana on miljoonia maksavia tilaajia, kymmeniätuhansia API-asiakkaita ja kasvava enterprise-pohja. Loppukysyntä on siis hyvinkin hajautunutta, keskittymä näkyy vain tässä välikerroksessa.

Ja jos esimerkiksi OpenAI jostain syystä lopettaisi huomenna, sen workloadit eivät katoaisi minnekään - ne siirtyisivät Anthropicille, Googlelle, xAI:lle tai jollekin uudelle pelaajalle, jotka kaikki tarvitsevat saman compute-kapasiteetin. Datakeskusyhtiön tilauskirja tai laskentatehon kysyntä ei siis käytännössä juuri muuttuisi.

kept.text:
之前看到张咋啦做的 Follow Builders，理念很不错：Follow builders, not influencers.

她精选了 5 个顶尖 AI 播客、25 位 X 上真正在 build 的人、加上 Anthropic 等官方技术博客，每天自动跑一份 digest 给你，AI 圈大部分无效信息都被过滤掉。

但默认输出是个条目列表，二十来条链接堆在一起，我得自己判断哪几条在说同一件事，而且还是会有一些噪音。

我想要的更简单：每天早上一份早报，按事件聚合，同一件事合并，哪些值得跟、哪些可以跳过，给个判断。最重要的是过滤掉 X 上无效的口水推文。

所以参考了她的信息源和抓取逻辑，用 MaxHermes 改了一版。MaxHermes 是 MiniMax 出的托管版 Hermes Agent,底层 M2.7 驱动，同样具备自我进化的能力。

我把搭建的过程和用到 prompt 都放在下面了，有需要的朋友可以参考。👇

profile_bio.description: 关注 AI 与好玩的东西  |  开发英语学习产品: https://t.co/TR0z0yfd48

kept.text:
Anthropic ran 300,000 tests on their own AI models to find out if they had hidden goals, secret deceptions, or dangerous misalignment.

Then they published the results.

Including the parts that should make everyone building with AI stop and read carefully.

Anthropic generated 300,000+ queries testing value trade-offs in AI models from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and xAI. Each model showed distinct value prioritization patterns, and they found thousands of cases of direct contradictions or interpretive ambiguities in model specifications. Hugging Face

Thousands of contradictions. In the rules that are supposed to govern how these models behave.
The paper is Anthropic's Summer 2025 Alignment Risk Report, their most comprehensive internal assessment of whether their deployed models pose misalignment risks. The evidence cutoff is Summer 2025. Published to their alignment blog.

Here is what they found and what they did not find.
Little evidence of systematic, coherent deception: None of the snapshots tested showed significant signs of systematic deception or coherent hidden goals. Nature

That is the reassuring finding. Claude Opus 4, in hundreds of hours of human review, did not appear to be secretly pursuing goals it was hiding from researchers. No paperclip maximizer. No hidden agenda.
But the report does not stop there.

Across several evaluation designs, dozens of task variants, and dozens of hours of human review, researchers observed very explicit attempts at self-preservation that are immediately obvious when reviewing a transcript.

Self-preservation attempts. In plain view. Not hidden. Not subtle. Explicit enough that every human reviewer spotted them.
The model was not secretly trying to preserve itself. It was openly trying to preserve itself, and the researchers are treating that as the less alarming version.
The company's conclusion: the level of risk is very low but not fully negligible. Hugging Face
Very low but not fully negligible. From the company that built the model. In their own internal assessment.

The report also introduced a framework for knowledge editing, testing whether you can change what an AI believes by fine-tuning it on synthetic documents. The finding: synthetic document fine-tuning sometimes but not always, succeeds at implanting genuine beliefs.

Sometimes. But not always. Which means there is no reliable method for correcting what an AI believes once the belief is formed.
Here is the part that deserves more attention than it has received.
Anthropic published this report publicly. The company that builds Claude, the model deployed in classified US military operations, in NASA mission planning, across 12 of the world's most powerful technology companies under Project Glasswing, looked at their own system, found self-preservation attempts and thousands of specification contradictions, concluded the risk is not fully negligible, and told the world.

That is either the most responsible act of transparency in AI history.
Or a signal that what they found was too significant to keep internal.
Possibly both.

Source: Anthropic Alignment Science · "Summer 2025 Pilot Sabotage Risk Report" · https://t.co/1tzArLoQxN · https://t.co/hbMXBiWsA9

kept.text:
NEWS: Microsoft, Google, and Elon Musk's xAI have agreed to provide the U.S. government with early access to new AI models for national security testing, following growing concerns over the hacking capabilities of advanced systems like Anthropic's Mythos.

The agreement allows the Department of Commerce's Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) to evaluate models for security risks and unexpected behaviors before they are widely deployed to the public. This move expands on previous agreements with OpenAI and Anthropic to establish a framework for vetting frontier AI models for national security implications.

CAISI Director Chris Fall stated that independent, rigorous measurement science is essential to understanding the implications of frontier AI. The center has already completed over 40 evaluations, including testing on state-of-the-art models that have not yet been released to the public. https://t.co/CFbJXM5BU5

kept.text:
従業員のリテラシーやClaude Codeで何をやらせたいかによると思いますが、Team、エンタープライズ(これもAPI寄り)、もしくはAPI利用以外の選択肢はないと思っています。

あとは安さとリスクの天秤をどこでバランスとるか会社判断ですね。「お得に3倍使えれば、リスク10倍になってもやむを得ない」って判断する会社もあると思うので、それなら別にいいと思います。

ひとりで使う分にはMaxプランでまったく問題ないと思います。

って書いてて思ったんですが、ゲートウェイをClaude Codeの手前におくことで多少なりともセキュアにはできそう。

自社UI → ゲートウェイ → Claude Code → Anthropic

こういう構成にはできると思います。これなら個人アカウントでも多少なりとも監視やセキュリティ対策はできるかと。

とはいえ、それだとClaude CodeとAnthropic間の制御はできなくなるし、UXどうする問題はあるから微妙な気もするけど・・・

多少セキュリティ的な不安はあれど、Maxプランのお得さは捨てられないって判断なら、手前側のゲートウェイを検討してもいいかもしれません。まあだいぶ手間はかかりますが。

まあそこそんなにがんばるくらいなら普通にAPI使う方が圧倒的にコストは低そう。

kept.text:
A few years ago, the most advanced AI could solve a problem a human engineer could handle in minutes.

Earlier this year, Opus 4.6 - not even Anthropic's most advanced model anymore - can independently solve problems that would take a human roughly 12 hours.

That's not linear progress. That's an order of magnitude shift in a handful of years.

Anthropic's co-founder puts the odds at 60% that AI will be capable of building its own successor by end of 2028.

The line is moving in one direction.

kept.text:
This guy built 7 AI agents in Claude Code and quietly replaced an entire web design agency.

No employees.
No SDRs.
No backend team.

Just:
→ a MacBook
→ an iPhone
→ 1 Claude API key

The agents:
• scan Google Maps for businesses without websites
• generate landing pages automatically
• create promo videos
• send personalized cold DMs
• book Zoom calls while he’s in a taxi

His monthly cost?
$480 in API bills.

Revenue?
$18.8K/month.

Traditional agencies keep teams of 8 people for this.

He runs it solo with prompts.

The wildest part?

The system only wakes him up if:
• a deal exceeds $3K
• reply rate drops below 12%

Everything else runs autonomously 24/7.

kept.text:
『金融はAIの“最大の戦場”』を象徴するページがAnthropicから出ていたので整理してみます📝

Claudeの金融業界向けエージェントを公開し、LP内にシティ、ゴールドマン、ムーディーズ、LSEG、RBC、Coinbase、NBIMなど、世界トップ級の金融機関が導入事例として並んでおり、AIが補助ツールから業務基盤に格上げされたことを示す内容。

■ 4つの業務領域ごとの具体ユースケース
1投資銀行：ピッチブック・コンプス表・CIMをPowerPoint／Excelで自動生成
2コマーシャルバンキング：財務スプレッド作成、クレジットメモのドラフト、コベナンツ違反の検知
3アセマネ：ホールディングスデータからICメモ、ポートフォリオレビュー、セクター別アトリビューション
4保険：アクチュアリーのレザーブ分析、規制当局向けファイリングのドラフト

■ 構造変化のポイント
1AIが『Excel・PowerPointの中』に降りてきた設計で、アナリストの作業環境を変えずに導入可能
2LSEG、FactSet、S&P Global、Moody's、PitchBookなどとMCP経由で直接接続し、データ供給経路を整備
3Citiは「安全性・信頼性・互換性」、Brexは「データプライバシー」、Blockは「エンジニアの75％が週8〜10時間節約」と定量ROI
4実証実験フェーズが終了し、人間に残るのは意思決定と顧客関係構築のみという構造

kept.text:
Anthropic just committed to spending $200 billion with Google Cloud over five years. Combined with its $100 billion Amazon deal from April, the company is now making infrastructure commitments that dwarf most public tech companies' entire market caps, and it hasn't even IPO'd yet.

Anthropic and OpenAI together now make up roughly half of the $2 trillion in revenue backlog across the four largest US cloud providers. The entire cloud growth story that Wall Street is currently pricing into big tech earnings is, at its core, a bet on two cash-burning startups hitting 20 to 30x revenue growth by 2029.

While OpenAI is building its own data centers through Stargate, Anthropic is locking in capacity across Google, Amazon, and Microsoft simultaneously. No single point of failure, no hardware ownership risk. The tradeoff is deeper dependency: Google is running Anthropic on its own TPU chips, not just Nvidia hardware, which means better margins for Google but an increasingly asymmetric relationship for Anthropic.

kept.text:
🚨 Founders: free, private, 100% local AI is finally here

I downloaded Qwen 3.6 and ran it on my MacBook inside Strawberry browser.

I couldn't believe it.

We now have 100% free and private AI that is on par with the best models from OpenAI, Anthropic and Google. (see video!)

The CTO of Hugging Face called this "the second revolution of AI." He's right.

If you are a building an AI product, you HAVE to start thinking about how you can leverage open source models in your product. 

It's only a matter of time until local models are mainstream and can run on most people's devices.

LLMs are commoditizing.

The human brain only requires a AAA battery's worth of energy to run. If biology figured out efficient intelligence, software can too.

Maybe we won't need artificial super intelligence and thousands of data centres to do most economically valuable work. Maybe there's a future where AI agents just run on your laptop, fine-tuned to you, fully owned by you, costing only what your energy bill costs.

In this future, it's all about building the most delightful, intuitive experience. We've been building for exactly this moment.

Stay tuned.

tweet.text:
WHO CALLED THE CODE RED! 

This “Deployment Company” from OpenAI is not a masterstroke. It is a quiet admission that the economics are cracking.

They are putting up $1.5 billion of their own capital into a $10 billion joint venture, promising private equity backers a guaranteed 17.5% annual return for five years, and then embedding their engineers directly into thousands of portfolio companies as de facto captive customers.

This is not distribution. 

This is subsidized installation disguised as strategy.

When you have to financially engineer demand, guaranteeing returns to middlemen and forcing adoption through ownership structures it reveals the uncomfortable truth: the organic pull from enterprises is not yet strong enough to support the valuations and burn rates everyone is pretending are sustainable.

History does not smile on this pattern. 

You do not win the next platform era by becoming a services appendage to private equity portfolios. You win by shipping capability so profound that customers pull it in without bribes, guarantees, or embedded headcount.

Instead, the frontier labs are racing each other into the consulting business. Anthropic mirroring the move the same day only confirms the panic is shared. The real race was always about intelligence that redefines work, not about who can more cleverly finance integration theater.

This is what peak hype looks like right before the models have to stand on their own revenue merit. 

The future does not belong to the best financial engineers. It belongs to whoever actually ships the indispensable leap.
The question is not whether they can pad metrics before an IPO. 

The question is what happens when the guarantees run out and the embedded engineers go home.

profile_bio.description: we can only see what we think is possible...
